AlphaLabX1 · internal research Vol. II

Valuation & Mood

S&P 500 and its eleven sectors, seen through two P/E lenses — and the market's mood, plotted against the price beneath it. Updated April 23, 2026.

valuation lens
Forward uses 12-month analyst estimates; trailing uses reported TTM earnings.
01

Where everyone stands today

Each marker is a sector's current P/E placed as a percentile of its own trailing five years. Right is expensive. A reading of 50 means the sector is trading at its own five-year median.

03

Historical path

Forward view shows 12-month analyst estimates (daily, since 2008). Trailing view uses reported TTM earnings (monthly, since 1995 for sectors — since 1871 for the index). The Y axis auto-scales to whichever window and series are visible.

04

Sentiment, at a glance

MacroMicro's Fear & Greed composite reduces the market's mood to a single 0–100 reading. Under 25 is panicked fear; over 75 is euphoric greed. The open triangles show how mood has drifted over the past week, month, quarter, and year.

composite · 0–100
extreme fear fear neutral greed extreme greed
▽1W90
▽1M20
▽3M61
▽1Y30
▼ 71 today · 2026-04-23
05

Mood against price

Sentiment on the left axis, S&P 500 on the right. Bear phases bottom with fear readings below 25; tops tend to coincide with extreme-greed plateaus — not coincidence, but also not a tradable signal on its own.

dual axis · shared X
